name: screenfull-fullscreen-api
description: Implement fullscreen functionality using screenfull library for cross-browser fullscreen support in ng-events project
qualityReason: Clear library and use case defined
capabilities: Add basic fullscreen toggle buttons to any component
Implement fullscreen mode for specific DOM elements
Create fullscreen image and video viewers
Build presentation mode with keyboard navigation
Clean up event listeners and manage fullscreen state
author: 7Spade
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing instructional markdown with code examples for using the screenfull library. No executable code, no network calls, no file system access, no environment variable reading. Purely a reference guide for implementing browser fullscreen API functionality.
valueStatement: Need cross-browser fullscreen support for your web application. This skill provides complete patterns for implementing fullscreen toggle buttons, immersive display modes, and presentation views using the screenfull library with Angular.
userTitle: Add fullscreen toggle to web apps
limitations: Does not include fullscreen API polyfill for legacy browsers
No built-in mobile touch gestures for fullscreen
Does not provide pre-built UI components or icons
iOS Safari has limited fullscreen support for video elements only

useCases.title: Build immersive dashboards
useCases.description: Create dashboard views that expand to fullscreen for better data visibility during presentations

useCases.title: Create media viewers
useCases.description: Build fullscreen image galleries and video players with smooth transitions and controls

promptTemplates.title: Basic fullscreen toggle
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a fullscreen toggle button to my Angular component using the screenfull library

promptTemplates.prompt: Create a fullscreen image viewer component that shows photos in immersive mode

bestPractices: Always check screenfull.isEnabled before using fullscreen API methods
Provide clear exit fullscreen buttons and handle Escape key properly
Clean up event listeners when components are destroyed to prevent memory leaks
antiPatterns: Do not assume fullscreen API is always available in all browsers
Avoid forcing fullscreen without user interaction or explicit button click
Do not ignore mobile browser limitations for fullscreen support

faq.question: Which browsers support screenfull.js?
faq.answer: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, and Android Chrome have full support. iOS Safari has limited support for video elements only.

faq.question: What is the minimum screenfull version required?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for screenfull version 6.0.2 or higher as specified in the package.json dependency.

faq.question: How do I check if fullscreen is available?
faq.answer: Use screenfull.isEnabled boolean property. Always check this before calling toggle, request, or exit methods.

faq.question: Does this skill send any data externally?
faq.answer: No. This is a documentation skill with code examples. The screenfull library runs entirely in the browser and does not transmit data.

faq.question: Why is fullscreen not working on mobile?
faq.answer: Mobile browsers have limited fullscreen support. iOS Safari only allows fullscreen for video elements. Test on desktop browsers for full functionality.

faq.question: How does screenfull differ from native Fullscreen API?
faq.answer: Screenfull provides cross-browser compatibility by normalizing browser differences and handling vendor prefixes automatically.
